data.title: Regencell Bioscience surges on apparent short squeeze
data.description:
• Regencell Bioscience (RGC)  is up ~35% on much higher than usual volume in what appears to be a short squeeze situation.
  • As of 225p ET, ~2.18M shares had traded hands. Average daily volume is ~151K.
  • Despite today's surge, Regencell is down ~61% year-to-date and is off ~44% over the last year.

data.title: At 252 years old, Birkenstock's growth story just getting started — Raymond James
data.description:
• Despite being founded more than 250 years ago, Raymond James analyst Rick Patel sees Birkenstock (BIRK) as a growth story with a revenue CAGR that easily outperforms softline peers.
  • Patel initiated coverage of the shoemaker with an Outperform as the current share price represents “a compelling growth opportunity at an attractive valuation.” The $52 price target represents 21% upside to Tuesday’s closing price
  • The bullish rating helped prop up shares, giving Birkenstock (BIRK) its first positive day in four days.
  • Singling out several specific growth levers, including increased production capacity, diversified assortment, expanded retail footprint, and extended relationships with wholesalers, Patel forecasts revenue growth of 11% in FY26 and 12.8% in FY27, translating into an estimated revenue CAGR of 12% through FY27 versus a peer average of 8%.
  • And as an affordable luxury brand with 90% full-price sell-through, Birkenstock (BIRK) enjoys industry-leading operating margins, Patel notes.

data.title: Phil Tseng to leave BlackRock TCP Capital CEO post - Bloomberg
data.description:
Phil Tseng is set to step down as CEO of BlackRock TCP Capital (TCPC), Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

BlackRock TCP is a publicly traded business development company, externally managed by an indirect subsidiary of the world's largest asset manager, BlackRock (BLK).

Tseng's departure date was unclear as of now, with the plan still not finalized, according to the sources who asked not to be identified discussing non-public information.

The private credit fund is said to have struggled after it marked down the net value of its assets twice in 2026—by 19% in January and 5% in May.

The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York, which encompasses the boroughs of Manhattan and the Bronx in New York City, was reportedly investigating the fund for its valuation practices.

Notably, a majority of publicly traded BDCs have turned unprofitable due to falling asset values and ​rising costs, an analysis by Reuters showed.

Loan losses and debt costs have jumped across 53 publicly traded BDCs, with a number of them utilizing ​more off-balance-sheet borrowing, according to an analysis of balance sheet data from S&P Global Market Intelligence.

Non-traded BDCs have recently attracted attention on surging redemption requests.

The Reuters report marks pressure building in the visible part of the private credit market.

Notable listed BDCs: (ARCC), (MAIN), (BXSL), (HTGC), (OBDC), (GBDC), (CSWC)

data.title: Most shorted consumer staple small caps dominated by Ridgetech, Beyond Meat
data.description:
The stocks with the highest short interest are concentrated in micro- and small-cap consumer-facing names, particularly within personal care products and packaged foods and agricultural companies dominating bearish positioning.

Among the most shorted stocks, Ridgetech (RDGT) leads with 70.1% of shares outstanding sold short, signaling deep skepticism around its financial outlook and business sustainability. Other most short names include Vital Farms (VITL) and Beyond Meat (BYND) with short interest of 28.55% and 26.61%, respectively.

In contrast, the least shorted names reflect relatively limited bearish positioning despite their smaller size and volatility.

Here are the five most shorted consumer staple stocks with market capitalizations of up to $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding):

  • Ridgetech (RDGT), Short interest: 70.11%.
  • Beyond Meat (BYND), Short interest: 28.55%.
  • Vital Farms (VITL), Short interest: 26.61%.
  • Grocery Outlet (GO), Short interest: 26.32%.
  • Upexi (UPXI), Short interest: 24.29%.

Here are the five least shorted consumer staple stocks with market capitalizations of up to $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding):

  • Benson Hill (BHILQ), Short interest: 0.56%.
  • Evercore Holdings (EVHO), Short interest: 0.63%.
  • MedAvail Holdings (MDVLQ), Short interest: 0.65%.
  • Caring Brands (CABR), Short interest: 0.70%.
  • Farmmi (FAMI), Short interest: 0.70%.

data.title: Defensive consumer staple giants remain least shorted, Freshpet tops bearish bets
data.description:
The stocks with the highest short interest come from a broad mix within the consumer staples sector, with Packaged Foods dominating the list in terms of positioning, according to June-end figures.

Among the most shorted stocks, Freshpet (FRPT) leads, just like the previous month, with 19.8% of shares outstanding sold short, signaling elevated skepticism around demand trends. Other of the most shorted names include Campbell's Company (CPB) and e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) with short percentages at 18.55% and 18.43%, respectively.

In contrast, the least shorted names reflect strong investor confidence and perceived stability. These least-shorted, famous names remain among the most defensively positioned. High market cap names that keep a hold on the least shorted list, including Brown-Forman (BF.A), Walmart (WMT), Coca-Cola (KO), Philip Morris (PM), and others.

Here are the five most shorted consumer staple stocks with market capitalizations above $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding):

  • Freshpet (FRPT), Short interest: 19.34%.
  • Campbell's (CPB), Short interest: 18.55%.
  • e.l.f. Beauty (ELF), Short interest: 18.43%.
  • Molson Coors Beverage (TAP), Short interest: 15.40%.
  • Post (POST), Short interest: 14.92%

Here are the five least shorted consumer staple stocks with market capitalizations above $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding):

  • Brown-Forman (BF.A), Short interest: 0.57%.
  • Walmart (WMT), Short interest: 1.05%.
  • Procter & Gamble (PG), Short interest: 1.06%.
  • Philip Morris International (PM), Short interest: 1.09%.
  • Coca-Cola (KO), Short interest: 1.19%.

data.title: Talos Energy raised to Buy at Roth after acquisition of 'very high margin' assets
data.description:
Talos Energy (TALO) up 4.1% in Wednesday's trading following its $850M acquisition of deepwater assets in the U.S. Gulf from Shell, which are expected to boost Talos' oil production by ~20% and be immediately accretive on key financial metrics.

Roth/MKM analyst Leo Mariani raised his rating on Talos (TALO) to Buy from Neutral with a $17 price target, up from $16, citing a "solid balance sheet, nice capital returns, low production declines, and below-average valuation."

Mariani says Talos (TALO) paid a relatively low purchase price of $30K per flowing boe for the U.S. Gulf assets, adding the company should be able to fund the acquisition entirely from its balance sheet with cash on hand and perhaps a small revolver draw.

The acquired assets have very low operating costs, which Talos (TALO) estimates at ~$5/boe, compared to $16/boe for the existing asset base, making the acquired assets very high margin, the analyst says.

Mariani also increases his cash flow per share estimate for 2026 and 2027 by 10% and 27%, respectively, due to higher cash flows from the acquisition.

data.title: Semi stocks overbought? Not according to Korea
data.description:
Despite growing market anxiety that the semiconductor sector (SMH) (SOXX) may be overbought, macro strategist Andreas Steno Larsen argued Wednesday the underlying fundamentals remain undeniably strong.

In a social media post, Larsen highlighted that every live metric he tracks for the sector is currently accelerating. Pointing specifically to South Korean (EWY) exports—historically a reliable coincident indicator for semiconductor stocks—he noted that recent figures "blew past all expectations."

A chart shared by Larsen illustrates a tight correlation between South Korea's (EWY) export growth and the performance of the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). Both metrics show a dramatic, near-vertical spike heading into the second half of 2026, backing his bullish stance.

data.title: SpaceX showed investors AI device prototype ahead of IPO: report
data.description:
• Ahead of last month's historic initial public offering, SpaceX (SPCX) showed investors  a prototype of a device for how people could interact and use artificial intelligence, The Wall Street Journal reported. 
  • The device, which is believed to run on Qualcomm's (QCOM) Snapdragon processors, is thinner than an Apple (AAPL) iPhone, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. It also used a new operating system and integrated AI tech from xAI, some of the people added.
  • SpaceX and Qualcomm did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Sphere will aim for the midnight crowd with Rocky Horror Show in Las Vegas
data.description:
Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) extended its strong 2026 rally over the last month after announcing that The Rocky Horror Picture Show at Sphere will be a major show at its Las Vegas venue in 2027. 

The Rocky Horror Picture Show franchise is a cult musical-comedy-horror universe that began as the stage musical The Rocky Horror Show in 1973 and the landmark 1975 film adaptation directed by Jim Sharman. Centered on the misadventures of naïve couple Brad and Janet in the castle of pansexual alien scientist Dr. Frank-N-Furter, it parodies mid-20th-century sci-fi and horror B-movies with glam-rock style and camp humor.

The franchise includes the original stage show, the film, the 1981 movie sequel Shock Treatment, plus numerous revivals, tours, and a 2016 TV remake. Disney (DIS) licensed the Rocky Horror rights to Sphere (SPHR) for the upcoming show.

Susquehanna analyst Joseph Stauff thinks Rocky Horror adds unique IP to its content portfolio that is likely to be targeted for later day-part start times vs. the earlier start times for its more global mass appeal of Wizard of Oz. 

"Importantly, we estimate this will be additive to the total number of original content shows with new start times likely to be in the ~10 p.m.-12 a.m. window. Currently, Wizard of Oz is running ~17 shows per week (at ~85% sell-through), and we raised our 2028 estimates assuming only about three more shows per week at 75% sell-through and ~67% contribution margin, given the more niche offering," highlighted Stauff.

Shares of Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) cooled off on Wednesday with a 2.1% swing lower but are still up more than 75% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: European regulator asks for third-party input in review of Electronic Arts' $55B takeover
data.description:
The European Commission is asking for third-party input as it reviews a consortium led by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund's planned $55 billion purchase of videogame maker Electronic Arts (EA). 

The Commission is inviting third parties to submit their observations on the proposed concentration to the regulator, according to a filing on Wednesday. The authority is asking for observations to reach the authority no later than 10 days following the date of the publication.

The buyer group filed with the European Commission last month, and the authority has until July 22 to decide to approve the transaction or move it into a more in-depth probe, according to a filing on the regulator's website. The acquisition will also see scrutiny under the European Union's foreign subsidy rules.

Private equity firm Silver Lake, Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, known as PIF, and Affinity Partners agreed to acquire Electronic Arts (EA) for $210 per share in cash in late September.
